name: Conveyor Line Tracking Study
description: An industrial automation engineer is programming a pick-and-place robot to grasp products dynamically from a moving conveyor belt without stopping the belt (Line Tracking). Validate the tracking algorithm's responsiveness in simulation using the scene messaging/movementViaRemoteApi.ttt. Write a Python script using the ZMQ Remote API to create a dummy object named conveyor_item at (0.0, 0.4, 0.2) and move it along the positive X-axis at a constant velocity of 0.05 m/s for 100 simulation steps in synchronous mode. Command the robot's IK target (named /target) to track the item with a Z-offset of 0.05 meters. Record the simulation time, positions of the item and the robot's end-effector tip (typically /IRB140_tip or /tip), and the Euclidean tracking error. Save the final scene to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/line_tracking_final.ttt. Export the time-series data to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/tracking_data.csv containing columns for step, time_s, item_x, item_y, item_z, tip_x, tip_y, tip_z, and error_m. Export a summary report to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/tracking_report.json containing fields for total_steps, simulated_duration_s, mean_error_m, min_error_m, max_error_m, and final_item_x.
